evals.prompt: Compile my Typst paper and tell me whether the layout looks obviously wrong for an IEEE-style submission.
evals.expected_output: Route to compile and format modules, run uv-backed Typst checks, and return Typst-oriented comments.

evals.prompt: Validate the bibliography in main.typ, whether it uses BibTeX or Hayagriva, and point out any missing citations.
evals.expected_output: Choose the bibliography module correctly and surface Typst citation/bibliography mismatches.

evals.prompt: Polish the abstract wording in my Typst paper and help me translate one Chinese paragraph into academic English without breaking labels.
evals.expected_output: Route to expression and translation modules while preserving Typst syntax.

evals.prompt: My Typst project uses a Hayagriva YAML bibliography. Validate it, then tell me whether any citations used in the paper are missing or unused.
evals.expected_output: Choose the bibliography path correctly for Hayagriva input, preserve Typst citation syntax, and surface missing, unused, or mismatched references.

evals.prompt: Review the related work section in main.typ for weak literature organization, missing gap derivation, and cross-section logic problems, but do not rewrite labels or math.
evals.expected_output: Route to the logic module, flag enumeration-heavy related work or missing gap statements, and keep Typst labels, cites, and math untouched.

evals.prompt: Check whether the introduction in my Typst paper jumps from broad background straight to our method without establishing the bottleneck and prior-work limitations.
evals.expected_output: Route to the logic module and flag introduction funnel breaks such as background-to-contribution jumps or missing literature-derived insufficiencies.

evals.prompt: Tell me whether the abstract, contribution claims, and conclusion in this Typst paper are aligned, or if they are saying different things.
evals.expected_output: Route to the logic module and report cross-section alignment issues involving problem, method, result, and contribution coverage.

evals.prompt: My Typst paper is in Chinese. Check the introduction for AI-sounding filler, repeated openings, and low-information paragraphs without breaking labels.
evals.expected_output: Route to the deai module with Chinese-aware heuristics and preserve Typst syntax while surfacing filler, parallel openings, and low-information density issues.

evals.prompt: Review the discussion section in main.typ and tell me if it is just a data dump without mechanism, comparison, limitations, or implications.
evals.expected_output: Route to the experiment module and flag shallow or unlayered discussion structure for a Typst paper.

evals.prompt: Review the algorithm-figure block in my IEEE-style Typst paper and tell me whether the caption, line numbers, and style-algorithm hook are in good shape.
evals.expected_output: Route to the pseudocode module, inspect algorithmic usage, and distinguish missing caption or wrapper issues from line-number recommendations.

evals.prompt: I used lovelace for a custom pseudocode block in main.typ. Tell me whether I still need a figure wrapper and caption for an IEEE-like submission.
evals.expected_output: Route to the pseudocode module, identify lovelace usage, and explain when figure wrapping and captions become mandatory for IEEE-like output.

evals.prompt: Rewrite the Related Work in my Typst paper so it sounds like a synthesis instead of a citation list. Keep every @cite anchor and show me the consensus-disagreement-gap chain.
evals.expected_output: Route to the literature module, preserve Typst citation anchors, diagnose missing comparison or gap derivation, and provide a rewrite chain from consensus to gap.
